steps.title: Track the word path
steps.content: Both examples show Indigenous words reaching English through Spanish: tomato comes from Nahuatl to Spanish to English, and maize comes from Taino to English through Spanish. Spanish is presented as an intermediary language.

steps.title: Reject unsupported claims
steps.content: Choice A adds contribution from Spanish to Indigenous languages, which the passage never says. Choice B compares vocabulary contribution amounts without evidence. Choice D turns two examples into an equal-number claim. The answer is C.
